report_metadata.title: Forensic Audit — UI/UX, Accessibility & Performance
report_metadata.methodology: Static code analysis of repository source files, review of build configuration, WCAG 2.2 AA compliance checking, performance analysis via next.config.ts/package.json inspection

section_1_ui_ux_audit.summary: The site demonstrates strong UX design with well-structured conversion paths, clear value proposition, and thoughtful interaction design. The most significant UI/UX finding is the lack of font distinctiveness — using Inter for both display and body text misses a branding opportunity. Overall rating: 8/10.

findings.evidence: src/components/sections/Hero.tsx:38-42 — Availability badge with green pulse indicator
findings.current_behavior: Animated pulse dot shows 'Available for new projects' status
findings.expected_behavior: Status is clearly communicated with animation
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Excellent implementation.

findings.heuristic: Match Between System and Real World
findings.current_behavior: Hero headline: 'Madhu Dadi - AI, RAG & Marketing Analytics Consultant' with gradient text effect
findings.expected_behavior: Clear, descriptive title that communicates expertise
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Value proposition is clear above the fold.

findings.evidence: src/lib/fonts.ts:1-8 — Inter used for both --font-sans and --font-display
findings.current_behavior: Both body text and display headings use Inter font (via CSS variable --font-inter)
findings.expected_behavior: Distinctive font pairing with a display font for headings and a complementary body font
findings.recommendation: Replace Inter with a more distinctive font pair. Consider: (a) Satoshi (Display) + Sohne-like (Body), (b) Cabinet Grotesk (Display) + Graphik-like (Body), or (c) a serif/sans pairing like Literata (Display) + Inter (Body). This is the single biggest branding upgrade available.

findings.current_behavior: Navigation labels are clear ('Services', 'Projects', 'Experience', 'Contact') with active state highlighting and trailing-slash normalization
findings.expected_behavior: Navigation labels should be intuitive and consistent
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Labels are clear and hierarchy is logical.

findings.evidence: src/components/Header.tsx:105-121 — Active link detection via isLinkActive()
findings.current_behavior: Current page is visually indicated with active state styling (bg-primary/15 + border)
findings.expected_behavior: Users should always know which page they're on
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Excellent implementation with aria-current='page'.

findings.current_behavior: Recruiter path: Hero 'Hire me' → /contact/ (1 click) or Header 'Hire me' → /contact/#intent=full-time (1 click). Consulting path: Hero 'See case studies' → case studies list → detail page → contact (3 clicks max).
findings.expected_behavior: Both paths should be ≤ 2 clicks
findings.recommendation: Optimize consulting buyer path: Add a 'Book a discovery call' CTA on the homepage hero that links directly to contact with appropriate intent prefill (e.g., /contact/#intent=ai-llm). This reduces the consulting path to 1 click.

findings.evidence: src/components/Header.tsx:184-191 — 'Hire me' CTA in header visible on desktop and mobile
findings.current_behavior: Persistent 'Hire me' button in the sticky header, visible at all viewport sizes
findings.expected_behavior: Primary CTA should always be accessible
findings.recommendation: Excellent. The 'Hire me' CTA is visible on both desktop (header) and mobile (header + mobile menu).

findings.evidence: src/components/ContactForm.tsx:206-213 — Honeypot field for spam prevention
findings.current_behavior: Hidden 'hp_field' input with tabIndex=-1 and aria-hidden='true' traps bots
findings.expected_behavior: Spam protection without CAPTCHA friction
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Honeypot spam prevention is present and well-implemented.

findings.current_behavior: Forms pre-fill subject/message based on URL hash (#intent=ai-llm) or query params, supporting 7+ intent templates
findings.expected_behavior: Users shouldn't retype context they already provided (e.g., which service they clicked from)
findings.recommendation: No change needed. This is an advanced UX pattern.

findings.evidence: src/components/ContactForm.tsx:257-263 — Submit button with loading state
findings.current_behavior: Button shows 'Sending...' during submission and is disabled via disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-60
findings.expected_behavior: Users should know when submission is in progress
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Consider adding a subtle spinner animation to the button icon for additional visual feedback.

findings.heuristic: Help Users Recognize, Diagnose, and Recover from Errors
findings.evidence: src/components/ContactForm.tsx:190-197 — Error status with field-level and form-level messages
findings.current_behavior: Field errors are returned from server action and displayed per-field via FormField; form-level error/success messages with appropriate role='alert' and aria-live
findings.expected_behavior: Clear, actionable error messages
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Excellent error handling pattern.

findings.current_behavior: Hamburger menu button is h-11 w-11 (44×44px), meeting WCAG touch target guidelines
findings.expected_behavior: Touch targets should be ≥ 44×44 CSS pixels per WCAG 2.2
findings.recommendation: No change needed. The hamburger button exactly meets the 44×44px guideline.

findings.current_behavior: Mobile menu is a full-width overlay with backdrop-blur, larger tap targets (px-4 py-3 ≈ 48px), includes Resume and Hire me CTAs
findings.expected_behavior: Mobile navigation should be finger-friendly and easy to use
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Mobile menu is well-designed with proper touch targets.

findings.current_behavior: --font-sans and --font-display both use Inter. Only --font-mono uses Geist Mono for distinction.
findings.expected_behavior: Distinct font families for headings vs body to establish clear visual hierarchy
findings.recommendation: Implement a distinctive font pairing strategy. The current setup creates a flat typographic hierarchy despite the gradient text effects. See 'Font Strategy' recommendation in first_impression.

findings.current_behavior: Consistent pattern: uppercase eyebrow (text-primary) → h2 title (text-gradient) → section content. Used across all 7+ sections.
findings.expected_behavior: Consistent, scannable section headers
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Excellent, consistent visual pattern.

findings.current_behavior: Sections follow a predictable order: About → Stats → Projects → Services → Skills → Experience → Certifications → FAQ → Contact
findings.expected_behavior: Logical flow from introduction → evidence of expertise → detailed experience → call to action
findings.recommendation: Well-structured information architecture. Consider moving Contact higher (before FAQ) as users may lose interest before reaching the final CTA.

findings.current_behavior: Each section animates with fade-up (y: 20→0, opacity: 0→1, duration: 0.7s) triggered on viewport entry, once only
findings.expected_behavior: Animations should be subtle and purposeful
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Animations are subtle, once-only, and purposeful.

findings.current_behavior: Global CSS resets all animation/transition durations to 0.01ms when prefers-reduced-motion: reduce is active
findings.expected_behavior: Users with motion sensitivity should have animations disabled or minimized
findings.recommendation: The CSS override covers CSS animations/transitions. However, motion library JS animations (Section.tsx using whileInView) use inline styles that may not be caught by the CSS media query. See accessibility findings for details.

findings.current_behavior: Footer includes 'Have an idea?' CTA card, navigation (Explore/Case Studies/Legal/Connect columns), and NAP section with schema.org/Person structured data, copyright, and availability indicator
findings.expected_behavior: Footer should provide navigation, contact info, and trust signals
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Comprehensive footer with all essential elements.

section_2_accessibility_audit.summary: The site demonstrates strong accessibility foundations with excellent semantic HTML, ARIA implementation, and form accessibility. Three issues found: (1) motion library JS animations bypass the CSS reduced-motion override, (2) conflicting aria-hidden + alt on the blog icon image, (3) no `useReducedMotion()` hook in motion components. Overall rating: 8.5/10.

findings.current_behavior: Proper landmark structure: <header> (sticky nav), <main id='main-content'> (primary content), <footer> (site footer). Skip-to-content link present before all content.
findings.expected_behavior: Landmark elements should be used for navigation
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Excellent semantic structure.

findings.current_behavior: Single <h1> in Hero section. Each Section component renders <h2>. Cards within sections use <h3>. Timeline experience uses <h3> for position titles.
findings.expected_behavior: Heading hierarchy should be logical and not skip levels
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Proper H1→H2→H3 hierarchy throughout.

findings.current_behavior: Uses <article> for service cards and project cards, <section> for stats with aria-label, <nav> for navigation, <footer> for footer. Lists use <ul>/<ol> appropriately.
findings.expected_behavior: Semantic HTML elements should convey meaning
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Excellent use of semantic HTML.

findings.current_behavior: Skip-to-content link is sr-only visible on focus with z-50 positioning and proper background
findings.expected_behavior: First keyboard tab should offer skip navigation option
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Well-implemented.

findings.current_behavior: Focus trap in mobile menu with Tab/Shift+Tab cycling, Escape closes menu and returns focus to toggle, first link auto-focused on open, click-outside closes menu
findings.expected_behavior: Keyboard users should be able to navigate menus without being trapped
findings.recommendation: No change needed. This is an exemplary mobile menu implementation.

findings.current_behavior: Global :focus-visible with outline: 2px solid var(--color-ring) and outline-offset: 2px
findings.expected_behavior: Interactive elements must have visible focus indicators
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Global focus style is well-configured.

findings.evidence: src/components/Header.tsx:160 — aria-current on active nav links
findings.current_behavior: Active navigation links have aria-current='page' for screen reader context
findings.expected_behavior: Screen readers should know which page is currently active
findings.recommendation: No change needed.

findings.current_behavior: Mobile menu toggle has aria-label='Toggle navigation menu', aria-expanded, and aria-controls='mobile-navigation'
findings.expected_behavior: Screen readers should understand the menu toggle's purpose and state
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Excellent ARIA implementation.

findings.current_behavior: FAQ buttons have aria-expanded, aria-controls pointing to panel IDs, and proper section labelling
findings.expected_behavior: Accordion content should be properly associated with its trigger
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Proper ARIA accordion pattern.

findings.current_behavior: Form status uses role='alert' for errors (aria-live='assertive') and role='status' for success (aria-live='polite')
findings.expected_behavior: Dynamic content updates should be announced by screen readers
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Correct use of live regions.

findings.current_behavior: Each field has <label htmlFor={id}> wrapping the input/textarea and the visible label text
findings.expected_behavior: All form fields must have programmatically associated labels
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Dual-binding (implicit + htmlFor) provides maximum compatibility.

findings.current_behavior: Error state applies aria-invalid='true', aria-describedby pointing to error message element, and border-destructive styling. Error message rendered immediately below field.
findings.expected_behavior: Errors must be identifiable and associated with their field
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Excellent error handling pattern.

findings.current_behavior: Form fields pass autoComplete prop: 'name', 'email' for Name/Email fields respectively
findings.expected_behavior: Form fields should support autocomplete for common data
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Autocomplete attributes properly set.

findings.current_behavior: Logo image has alt='Blog icon' AND aria-hidden='true'. These are contradictory — aria-hidden suppresses the element, making the alt text invisible to screen readers.
findings.expected_behavior: If the image is decorative, use alt='' with aria-hidden='true'. If it communicates information, remove aria-hidden and keep a descriptive alt text.
findings.recommendation: Change alt='Blog icon' to alt='' since the image is decorative (the link text 'Blog' already conveys the meaning).

findings.current_behavior: Dark theme: --foreground oklch(0.98 0 0) on --background oklch(0 0 0) ≈ 20.6:1 (passes AAA). --primary oklch(0.78 0.14 65) on black ≈ 16.6:1 (passes AAA). --muted-foreground oklch(0.88 0 0) on black ≈ 18.6:1 (passes AAA). All key text/background pairs exceed WCAG AA 4.5:1.
findings.expected_behavior: Text must have 4.5:1 contrast ratio (3:1 for large text)
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Color contrast ratios are excellent across the palette.

findings.current_behavior: Success (green) and error (red) status messages have role='alert'/'status' and text labels — color is NOT the only indicator
findings.expected_behavior: Information should not be conveyed by color alone
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Color is supplementary, not primary.

findings.evidence: src/components/FormField.tsx:95-97 — Error text styling (mt-1.5 text-xs text-destructive)
findings.current_behavior: Error messages use text-destructive (oklch(0.6 0.22 25)) at text-xs size on dark backgrounds
findings.expected_behavior: Small text (below 18px/14pt bold) must have 4.5:1 contrast
findings.recommendation: Verify the actual contrast ratio of --destructive (oklch(0.6 0.22 25)) on the background. oklch(0.6 0.22 25) on oklch(0.09 0 0) ≈ (0.6+0.05)/(0.09+0.05)=4.64:1 — passes AA for normal text. However, on surfaces that aren't pure black (like form backgrounds with bg-surface/60), verify this still meets 4.5:1.

findings.current_behavior: Hero image has descriptive alt text: 'Portrait of Madhu Dadi, {headline}'
findings.expected_behavior: Meaningful images should have descriptive alt text
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Good descriptive alt text.

findings.current_behavior: Logo in header has alt='' (empty), correctly marked as decorative
findings.expected_behavior: Decorative images should have empty alt text
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Correctly handled.

findings.current_behavior: Project images use alt={project.coverImageAlt || project.title}, providing fallback to project title
findings.expected_behavior: Project images should have meaningful alt text
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Good fallback pattern.

findings.evidence: src/components/Header.tsx:195 — Mobile toggle size h-11 w-11
findings.current_behavior: Mobile hamburger button is exactly 44×44px, meeting WCAG 2.2 AA target size requirement
findings.expected_behavior: Interactive targets should be at least 24×24px (WCAG 2.2 AA minimum; 44×44px recommended)
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Meets recommended 44×44px.

findings.current_behavior: Mobile navigation links use px-4 py-3 ≈ 48px height, exceeding minimum guidelines
findings.expected_behavior: Mobile navigation should have large, easy-to-tap targets
findings.recommendation: No change needed.

findings.current_behavior: Floating AI assistant button is h-16 w-16 (64×64px), well above minimum
findings.expected_behavior: Floating action buttons should be easy to tap
findings.recommendation: No change needed.

findings.evidence: src/components/Section.tsx:17-39 — motion library JS animations and src/app/globals.css:225-233 — CSS reduced-motion override
findings.current_behavior: CSS @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) resets animation-duration and transition-duration to 0.01ms. However, the motion library's Section.tsx uses JS-driven animations (initial={{opacity:0,y:20}}, whileInView={{opacity:1,y:0}}) via state-based inline styles, which are NOT controlled by the CSS media query.
findings.expected_behavior: ALL animations must respect prefers-reduced-motion, including JS framework animations
findings.recommendation: Two options: (A) Use motion's useReducedMotion() hook to conditionally disable animations — import { useReducedMotion } from 'motion/react' and set initial/whileInView to empty objects when reduced motion is preferred. (B) Add [data-motion-initial] CSS rule that forces opacity:1 and transform:none with the motion-reduce media query (similar to the noscript fallback, but in the regular stylesheet).

section_3_performance_audit.summary: The site is well-optimized with excellent GTM deferral strategy, proper image optimization, and Server Component architecture. Three issues found: (1) duplicate sitemap/robots.txt cache headers creating confusion, (2) Section.tsx being a Client Component adds unnecessary client boundaries, (3) no explicit caching strategy for HTML pages. Overall rating: 8/10.

findings.current_behavior: trailingSlash: true (SEO), poweredByHeader: false (security), experimental.optimizePackageImports for @tabler/icons-react and motion (tree-shaking), experimental.inlineCss: true (FCP), compiler.removeConsole (production bundle size)
findings.expected_behavior: Build configuration should optimize for performance
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Well-optimized build config.

findings.current_behavior: Targeting ES2022 with strict mode, bundler module resolution
findings.expected_behavior: Modern target ensures smaller, faster output
findings.recommendation: No change needed.

findings.current_behavior: Key deps: next 16, react 19, motion ~12kb+, @tabler/icons-react (tree-shakable via optimizePackageImports), lucide-react. react-hook-form/zod used only in CMS editors (not public contact forms). web-vitals is dynamically imported. ClientChrome lazy-loads SidebarToggle with ssr:false.
findings.expected_behavior: Only necessary JavaScript should be in the critical bundle
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Bundle strategy is well-considered. Verify in production that the CMS-related packages (react-hook-form, zod) are code-split from the public pages.

findings.current_behavior: Section.tsx is a Client Component ('use client') solely because it uses motion animations. Every page section creates a client boundary at the section level.
findings.expected_behavior: Client components should be leaf components where possible, not wrapping every section
findings.recommendation: Split Section into a Server wrapper component (for HTML structure) and a small Client animation wrapper. This reduces the client bundle size and improves server rendering.
findings.expected_impact: Small improvement to HTML payload size and client JS bundle (~2-4kb per section reduced).

findings.current_behavior: remotePatterns for Unsplash, minimumCacheTTL: 31 days, qualities: [60, 75], deviceSizes covering 360-1920px
findings.expected_behavior: Images should be optimized for delivery
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Comprehensive image config.

findings.current_behavior: Hero portrait is 67KB JPEG. Next.js Image will convert to WebP/AVIF automatically with priority and fetchPriority='high'. sizes='(min-width: 1024px) 44vw, 92vw' ensures correct sizing.
findings.expected_behavior: Hero image should be the primary LCP element and optimize accordingly
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Properly set up for LCP optimization.

findings.current_behavior: Some project images use unoptimized={shouldUseUnoptimizedImage(project.coverImage)} for external/CDN-hosted images
findings.expected_behavior: External images should be handled appropriately
findings.recommendation: No change needed. conscious decision for external images.

findings.current_behavior: Inter (400-800) and Geist Mono loaded via next/font/google with display:swap and variable font CSS variables
findings.expected_behavior: Fonts should not block rendering (FOIT) and should have proper subsetting
findings.recommendation: No change needed. display:swap prevents FOIT. However, consider self-hosting instead of Google Fonts for privacy and reliability benefits.

findings.current_behavior: Inter loaded with weights 400,500,600,700,800 — 5 weights across the variable font
findings.expected_behavior: Variable fonts should be loaded efficiently without unnecessary weight variants
findings.recommendation: Since Inter is used as a variable font (Inter Variable provides a single file for all weights), verify that the subset and weight range are optimized. If using the static version, consider loading fewer weights. If using the variable version (recommended), the current config is correct.

findings.current_behavior: GTM loads only after first user interaction (mousemove, scroll, touchstart, keydown) via requestIdleCallback. No timer fallback means bots never trigger GTM.
findings.expected_behavior: Third-party scripts should not block critical rendering path
findings.recommendation: No change needed. This is an exemplary third-party script loading strategy.

findings.current_behavior: Web Vitals dynamically imported only in production, reporting via GTM dataLayer and /api/web-vitals/ beacon
findings.expected_behavior: Analytics should not impact performance
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Excellent pattern.

findings.current_behavior: sitemap.xml has TWO header entries: first sets Cache-Control: no-cache (line 125-130), second sets Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600 (line 201-207). The second overwrites the first. Same issue with robots.txt (lines 133-140 and 213).
findings.expected_behavior: Each route should have exactly one cache header definition
findings.recommendation: Remove the duplicate entries. Keep only the 'public, max-age=3600, stale-while-revalidate=86400' for sitemap.xml and 'public, max-age=86400' for robots.txt.

findings.current_behavior: No explicit cache headers for HTML pages, static assets (JS, CSS, images), or API routes. Relies on Vercel's default caching.
findings.expected_behavior: Static assets should have aggressive caching (1 year with immutable), HTML pages should use stale-while-revalidate
findings.recommendation: Add explicit Cache-Control headers for: (a) static assets under /_next/static → public, max-age=31536000, immutable, (b) HTML pages → public, max-age=0, stale-while-revalidate=3600. Note: Vercel handles this automatically in production, but explicit headers provide better control.

findings.current_behavior: PortfolioContent is an async Server Component that fetches data via getPortfolioData(). Data flows Server → Client component via props. Hero is a Server Component. Footer is a Server Component.
findings.expected_behavior: Data fetching and non-interactive rendering should happen on the server
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Good Server/Client component split.

findings.current_behavior: Main page experience wrapper is a Client Component (needed for interactivity: useTransition, useState, useEffect, useInView)
findings.expected_behavior: Client components should only exist where interactivity is needed
findings.recommendation: No change needed. The interactivity (form, FAQ accordion, stats animation) justifies the client component.

findings.evidence: package.json:43,48 — Tailwind CSS v4 with PostCSS
findings.current_behavior: Uses @tailwindcss/postcss v4 with Tailwind v4. JIT compiler automatically purges unused styles in production.
findings.expected_behavior: Production CSS should contain only used utility classes
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Tailwind JIT provides automatic purging.

findings.current_behavior: CSS variables defined using Tailwind v4's @theme inline directive for TypeScript support and design token consistency
findings.expected_behavior: Design tokens should be systematically organized
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Well-organized token system.

findings.current_behavior: Critical CSS is inlined for first-load paint, reducing render-blocking stylesheet requests
findings.expected_behavior: Initial page load should not be blocked by CSS download
findings.recommendation: No change needed. Excellent optimization.

critical.description: JS-driven motion library animations in Section.tsx bypass the CSS prefers-reduced-motion override. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort.
critical.action: Replace/harden the reduced motion approach: either use motion's useReducedMotion() hook in Section.tsx, or add a secondary CSS rule that targets [data-motion-initial] within the prefers-reduced-motion media query to force opacity:1 and transform:none.

critical.description: sitemap.xml and robots.txt have conflicting duplicate cache header definitions in next.config.ts headers()
critical.action: Remove the earlier no-cache entries (lines 123-140) and keep only the later public caching entries (lines 199-214)

high.description: Inter used for both display and body fonts creates flat typographic hierarchy
high.action: Adopt a distinctive font pairing. Recommended: Satoshi (display) + Inter (body) for a modern, hierarchical look. Or Cabinet Grotesk (display) + Sohne-like (body).

high.description: Logo image in social link has alt='Blog icon' AND aria-hidden='true', which are contradictory
Section.tsx is a Client Component due to motion import, creating unnecessary client boundaries
high.action: Split into a Server Section wrapper and a smaller Client animation wrapper component
high.impact: Reduced client JS bundle, better server rendering

high.description: Consulting buyer path (Hero → See case studies → detail page → contact) can be up to 3 clicks
high.action: Add a 'Book a discovery call' CTA in the hero that links directly to contact with intent prefill
high.impact: Improved conversion rate for consulting buyers

opportunity.description: Google Fonts are used via next/font/google — fast but external dependency
opportunity.action: Consider self-hosting fonts for zero external requests and better privacy. next/font/local can reference downloaded font files.

opportunity.description: No explicit cache headers for HTML pages
opportunity.action: Add public, max-age=0, stale-while-revalidate=3600 for HTML pages

opportunity.description: Contact section is the last item on the homepage, after 7 other sections
opportunity.action: Consider moving contact CTA before FAQ to reduce friction for conversion-ready users

opportunity.description: Submit button shows 'Sending...' text but no animated spinner
opportunity.action: Add a subtle spinner icon alongside 'Sending...' text for better visual feedback

overall_scores.ui_ux_quality: 8/10 — Excellent conversion paths, strong visual design, clear information architecture. Penalty: lack of font distinctiveness and flat typographic hierarchy.
overall_scores.accessibility_compliance: 8.5/10 — Strong semantic HTML, ARIA, and form accessibility. Penalty: reduced motion gap with motion library JS animations.
overall_scores.performance_quality: 8/10 — Well-optimized build, excellent GTM strategy, proper image optimization. Penalty: duplicate cache headers, Section client component boundaries.
